description: This figure presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) characterization of Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–xBe (x = 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5) low-density steels hot-rolled at 1050 °C, arranged in a 4×4 matrix. Rows (a)–(d) correspond to increasing Be content (0Be, 0.5Be, 1.0Be, and 1.5Be), while columns display distinct EBSD mappings: Column 1 shows Image Quality (IQ) maps with grain boundaries color-coded by misorientation angles (blue >15°, green 5°–15°, red 1°–5°); Column 2 presents Inverse Pole Figure (IPF) orientation maps revealing crystallographic textures relative to the rolling direction (RD); Column 3 displays Kernel Average Misorientation (KAM) maps indicating local stress concentrations, particularly at carbide and Be–Fe phase precipitates located at grain boundaries and phase interfaces; and Column 4 shows phase maps where green denotes austenite and red indicates ferrite. The microstructural evolution demonstrates a transition from single-phase austenite (0Be) to dual-phase austenite-ferrite structures (1.0Be and 1.5Be) with increasing Be content, accompanied by grain refinement and altered precipitation distributions. The KAM maps reveal that stress concentration becomes more uniformly distributed in higher-Be alloys (1.0Be, 1.5Be), correlating with the formation of ferrite and Be-containing intermetallic phases.
